potential_reason: Summer 2021 low-ratio period coincident with the recurring spring/summer signature seen across ALL Belgian offshore farms on the Modular Offshore Grid (Belwind, Nobelwind, Northwind, Rentel, Northwester 2, Thornton Bank). The MOG itself reported 99.998-100% availability 2020-2023, so this is not a hub outage. Most likely a mix of Belgian-wide downward regulation/curtailment during low-load, high-renewable, frequently negative-price summer periods, with a possible empirical-power-curve seasonal (ERA5 wind) bias inflating the expected baseline. Recommend team check Elia curtailment/redispatch logs for the window.
source_name: Elia - Summer Outlook 2025 (curtailment of offshore wind in negative-price periods)
evidence_note: Same calendar window recurs across all 7 unrelated Belgian farms; MOG availability 99.998-100% 2020-2023 rules out hub fault. Both q50 and q90 depressed. Possible data-artifact overlay; verify against Elia downward-regulation data.

potential_reason: Mar-Apr 2022 window is shared verbatim by Nobelwind, Northwester 2, Northwind, Rentel, SeaMade and Thornton Bank. A simultaneous identical drop across all Belgian MOG farms (MOG ~100% available in 2022) points to a systemic cause: Belgian-wide downward regulation during spring negative-price/oversupply hours, or a spring ERA5 wind-speed overestimate in the empirical curve. Not a Belwind-specific fault.

evidence_note: Identical Mar 1 - Apr 30 2022 window across 7 farms = systematic, not per-asset. Strong candidate for data artifact (seasonal power-curve bias) or fleet curtailment. Flag for diagnostics review.
